title: Are You Ready for a New Pet?
intro: Answer 7 quick questions to see how well a new pet fits your time, space, budget, and daily routine right now. This is a fun self-reflection tool for general education, not veterinary advice.

questions.q: How much hands-on time can you realistically give a pet every single day?

options.t: A solid chunk - I'm home often and happy to build a routine
Some, though my schedule is busy and a bit unpredictable
Honestly very little right now between work and life

questions.q: How do you feel about the messier parts - cleaning litter, bagging waste, fur, or the occasional accident?

options.t: No problem, it comes with the territory
I can handle it, but I'd rather keep it minimal
That part really makes me hesitate

questions.q: How would an unexpected vet bill of a few hundred dollars land for you?

options.t: I keep a cushion set aside for exactly this kind of thing
It'd be tight but I could manage it
That would be a serious strain right now

questions.q: How settled is your living situation for the next several years?

options.t: Stable, pet-friendly, and I expect it to stay that way
Mostly steady, with a few unknowns ahead
In flux - I'm not sure where I'll be

questions.q: How does your household feel about adding a pet?

options.t: Everyone's on board and excited to share the care
Generally supportive, though it's mostly on me
It's not really discussed or there's some pushback

questions.q: How do you picture handling travel, long days out, or being away?

options.t: I've got sitters, family, or a plan already in mind
I'd figure it out as situations come up
I travel a lot and coverage would be tough

questions.q: What's drawing you to get a pet right now?

options.t: A long-term companion I'm ready to learn about and care for
Companionship, though I'm still weighing the commitment
A spur-of-the-moment urge or a gift idea

results.title: A new pet might not be your easiest fit just yet
results.body: Your answers suggest a few pieces of the puzzle - time, budget, stability, or household buy-in - aren't quite lined up right now, and that's completely okay. Many great pet owners started exactly here. Consider easing in: fostering, pet-sitting for a friend, or volunteering at a shelter lets you enjoy animals while life settles. It also tells you a lot about what you'd actually want.
results.cta: Read our 'before you adopt' starter guide

results.title: A new pet could work well for you, with a little planning
results.body: You have several of the building blocks in place, and a few areas would just benefit from a plan - maybe a small care fund, a pet-sitting backup, or a chat with your household about who does what. Get those squared away and you'll be in a really strong spot. Matching the right species and energy level to your routine is the next smart move.
results.cta: Compare beginner-friendly pets and their needs

results.title: You're in a strong spot for a new pet
results.body: Your time, space, budget, and mindset all point to real readiness, and your reasons are thoughtful and long-term. That's the foundation a happy pet needs. Now you can focus on the fun, important details: choosing the right match, preparing your home, and lining up that first wellness visit so you start off on the right paw.
results.cta: Explore our new-pet setup and first-vet-visit guides

disclaimer: This quiz is for general education and fun, not veterinary or medical advice. Every animal and household is different - please consult your veterinarian before making decisions about a pet's care or your readiness to adopt.
